intro: Casparia coulteri, commonly known as Coulter's casia or Coulter's casparia, is a plant species native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, offering health benefits such as anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects, which may aid in the treatment of wounds, infections, and digestive issues. Bioactive constituents of Casparia coulteri include coumarins, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties.
